preamble: Nasal palatalization occurs when an alveolar nasal [n] shifts to the palatal nasal [ɲ]. This is a standard historical process in Romance languages (e.g., Latin 'vinea' > Spanish 'viña' [ɲ]) and Slavic languages, typically triggered by a following high front vowel or palatal glide [j]. This process is central to the slender/broad contrast in Goidelic languages.

examples.note: Slender/broad contrast where /n/ is realized as palatalized [ɲ] in specific phonological environments.
Synchronic assimilation where /n/ and /j/ merge into [ɲ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
